mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid monthly decline in the unemployment rate signals accelerating labor demand and wage growth, which boosts consumer spending expectations and drives equity valuations higher over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances across a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in residential housing starts and consumer credit balances indicates synchronized household balance sheet expansion, which amplifies aggregate demand and sustains positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 150 basis points over any rolling 12-month period.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index exceeds 105 during a mature economic cycle, it indicates capacity utilization peaks that historically precede margin compression and earnings downgrades, depressing equity prices over the following month.
failure_condition: Industrial production above 105 consistently correlates with positive S&P 500 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of observations over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP reflects underlying economic momentum that translates directly into higher corporate earnings, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 returns over a three-month forward window.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth rates above the 20-year median fail to generate statistically significant positive 60-day forward returns (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price levels combined with a federal funds rate below 4.0 percent create a disinflationary monetary environment that supports real yield compression and equity risk premium expansion, driving positive monthly returns.
failure_condition: The interaction signal yields a negative information coefficient in more than 40% of rolling 24-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market tightening cycle, which historically precedes broad-based equity rallies over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal produces a Sharpe ratio below 0.2 over any 3-year out-of-sample evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in housing starts below 1.4 million units signals a contraction in residential investment and household wealth effects, which drags down aggregate demand and generates negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Housing starts below 1.4 million units correlate with positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances across a 10-year test.
